title: festuca laevis subsp. gallica
intro: Festuca laevis subsp. gallica, also known as a subspecies of the smooth fescue grass, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Festuca laevis subsp. gallica include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
